findings.evidence: `rg` shows the new theorem at `PathSpace/Shift.lean:63` and the existing helper at `Exchangeability/Contractability.lean:134`: `((pathLaw μ X).map (fun x => fun k => x (φ k))).map (prefixProj α n) = blockLaw μ X (fun i => φ i.val)`; with `φ := fun k => k + r` and `shift_iterate_apply`, it gives the same finite block law.
findings.fix: Expose/reuse the generic reindex-prefix path-law lemma, or derive this result only as a local corollary where needed rather than adding a public shift-specific theorem.
findings.issue: `map_prefixProj_shift_iterate_pathLaw` is the `φ k = k + r` special case of the existing generic prefix-reindex path-law lemma, creating a parallel shift-only finite-block API.

summary: The PR still adds a shift-specific finite-block path-law theorem that overlaps the existing generic reindexing helper instead of exposing/reusing the general form.
